sanskrit:
सगाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन।
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम्॥
transliteration:
sargāṇām ādir antaś ca madhyaṁ caivāham arjuna |
ādhyātma-vidyā vidyānāṁ vādaḥ pravadatām aham ||
literal_meaning: Of all creations I am the beginning, the end and the middle, O Arjuna; of sciences I am the Self-knowledge, and among debaters I am the conclusive truth.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that every phase of any process—birth, sustenance, dissolution—already rests within a single, coherent Self.  By claiming to be the definitive conclusion of every argument, he collapses the anxious need to ‘figure everything out’ and offers a felt-sense of containment that precedes thought.
psychological_state: Post-epiphany integration: the mind stops scanning for beginnings or endings and experiences the present as already complete.
trigger_scenario: A perfectionist coder finally ships version 1.0, realizes the code will be refactored, deprecated and rewritten—yet senses their own awareness as the unbroken thread through every iteration.
anchor_text: Rest in the Self that already holds every beginning, middle, and end.
